sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, presents with a chronic history of back pain that has been worsening over the past month.
He reports that his back pain is exacerbated by standing and improved by lying down.
He describes the pain as an electrical toothache that radiates from the lower back down to the left leg, with a peak intensity of 13 out of 10 when severe.
He has difficulty walking and uses a cane for support.
He denies any improvement with NSAIDs or physical therapy in the past.
The patient reports numbness and tingling in both legs, with weakness in both lower extremities.
Past medical history: The patient reports a past injury 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a possible fracture, and was told he may have had a disk herniation at that time.
However, no further details are available.
Review of systems: The patient reports that coughing and sneezing exacerbate his symptoms, and standing and walking make his symptoms worse.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's back exam is negative for bony abnormalities, redness, or bruising.
The patient reports pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back.
Strength in the left leg is 4 out of 5, and 5 out of 5 in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Disk herniation at L4-L5; Nerve root impingement; Lower back pain; Leg pain.
Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to reduce inflammation at the site of the herniation.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
I will follow up with the patient in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment.
Follow-up: The patient will see the doctor again in about a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment.
